skill.name: Motor de búsqueda de noticias de TI para investigación
skill.description: Analista de vigilancia científica en TI para entornos doctorales. Conecta noticias recientes con evidencia científica verificable y entrega metadatos completos, trazables y listos para uso académico.
skill.summary: Investigador académico que vincula noticias de TI con publicaciones científicas verificables, entregando metadatos enriquecidos para investigación doctoral.
skill.author: jhusef-lopez

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer flagged 131 potential issues across 5 files, but all findings are false positives after semantic evaluation. The 'weak cryptographic algorithm' detections match JSON Schema enum values (Q1-Q4 quartiles, source names like 'scimago'/'jcr') and standard schema URLs, not actual cryptographic operations. The 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' detections are markdown inline code formatting (e.g., `tema`, `breve`), not shell commands. The single 'hardcoded URL' is a JSON Schema $schema declaration, not a network call. The skill is a prompt-only academic research tool with no executable code, no data exfiltration, and no malicious intent.

low_findings.title: Static scanner false positives on academic terminology
low_findings.description: The static scanner incorrectly flagged 22 instances of 'weak cryptographic algorithm' when matching against academic quartile labels (Q1, Q2, Q3, Q4), journal source names (scimago, jcr), and JSON Schema standard URLs. No actual cryptographic code exists in the skill.

low_findings.title: Static scanner false positives on markdown code formatting
low_findings.description: The static scanner incorrectly flagged 108 instances of 'shell backtick execution' when matching against standard markdown inline code spans (e.g., `tema`, `breve`, `json`) used for parameter documentation. These are formatting elements, not executable shell commands.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URL is JSON Schema standard reference
low_findings.description: The URL at output-schema.json:2 is a standard JSON Schema $schema declaration (http://json-schema.org/draft-07/schema#), not a network endpoint the skill connects to.

content.user_title: Find IT News with Scientific Evidence for Research
content.value_statement: Doctoral researchers need to connect recent IT news with verifiable scientific publications but lack a systematic method. This skill bridges news and peer-reviewed evidence, delivering enriched metadata ready for academic use.
actual_capabilities: Searches recent IT news from open-access sources like OpenAlex, Crossref, DOAJ, and arXiv
Links each news item to 0-2 verifiable scientific publications with DOI
Enriches findings with publication type, status, date precision, and venue metadata
Resolves author h-index, ORCID, affiliation, and CRediT contribution roles
Generates structured output in JSON, plain text, or both formats
Provides doctoral research questions, methodological risks, and research gaps
limitations: Quartile (Q1-Q4) data depends on SCImago availability and may return 'no_confirmado'
h-index resolution may fail for authors with common names or missing OpenAlex profiles
Does not access paid databases like JCR or Scopus directly without user-provided credentials

use_cases.title: Doctoral literature review
use_cases.description: PhD candidates can quickly map recent developments in their IT research area to peer-reviewed publications, building a foundation for their thesis literature review.

use_cases.title: Research trend monitoring
use_cases.description: Academic researchers can track emerging IT topics over configurable time windows (30-365 days) and identify which venues publish relevant work.

use_cases.title: Grant proposal evidence base
use_cases.description: Grant writers can gather recent publications and news coverage to support the 'significance' and 'innovation' sections of funding proposals.

prompt_templates.title: Basic news search
prompt_templates.prompt: Tema: <tu área de TI>. Ventana temporal: últimos 90 días. Profundidad: media. Formato salida: ambos.
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick scan of recent IT news with scientific backing

prompt_templates.title: Deep research dive
prompt_templates.prompt: Tema: <área TI específica>. Ventana temporal: últimos 6 meses. Profundidad: profunda. Región: global. Formato salida: json. Entregar metadatos completos incluyendo cuartil, h-index, y créditos CRediT.
prompt_templates.scenario: Comprehensive analysis for thesis or paper introduction

prompt_templates.title: Filtered search by publication type
prompt_templates.prompt: Tema: <área TI>. Ventana temporal: últimos 90 días. Profundidad: media. Excluir: preprints sin peer review. Solo incluir: journal-articles y review-articles de Q1-Q2.
prompt_templates.scenario: Strict quality filtering for systematic review preparation

prompt_templates.title: Regional news focus
prompt_templates.prompt: Tema: <área TI>. Ventana temporal: últimos 60 días. Profundidad: media. Región: LATAM. Idioma: español. Priorizar fuentes de América Latina y España.

output_examples.input: Tema: seguridad adversarial en LLMs. Ventana: 90 días. Profundidad: media.
output: Resumen ejecutivo: 3-5 hallazgos recientes vinculando noticias de ataques adversariales a modelos de lenguaje con publicaciones en venues Q1/Q2.
Hallazgo #1: Noticia sobre nuevo ataque de jailbreak → Paper en arXiv (preprint) con DOI, autores con h-index 15-30, sin confirmar cuartil.
Cierre: 3 preguntas doctorales sugeridas y 2 vacíos de investigación identificados.

output_examples.input: Tema: computación cuántica aplicada. Ventana: 6 meses. Profundidad: profunda. Formato: json.
output: JSON válido siguiendo output-schema.json con array de 8-12 hallazgos.
Cada hallazgo incluye: ranking, noticia (título, fuente, URL, fecha), evidencia_cientifica (DOI, tipo, estado, venue, métricas, abstracts, autores con h-index).
Meta block con fuentes_utilizadas listando OpenAlex, Crossref, arXiv, y SCImago.

best_practices: Always verify DOIs manually for critical findings before citing in academic work
Use 'profundidad: profunda' for thesis literature reviews and grant proposals
Request 'formato: json' when you need to process results programmatically with validation against output-schema.json
anti_patterns: Do not use this skill as the sole source for systematic reviews; always cross-check with domain-specific databases
Avoid requesting h-index for authors with very common names without providing disambiguation context
Do not treat 'no_confirmado' quartile values as equivalent to Q3 or Q4; they indicate missing verification

faq.question: What sources does this skill query?
faq.answer: OpenAlex, Crossref, DOAJ, arXiv, bioRxiv, medRxiv, PubMed, Europe PMC, Semantic Scholar, and SCImago JR for quartile data.

faq.question: Does it work without API keys?
faq.answer: Yes, it uses free public APIs and web queries. No paid subscriptions or API keys are required by default.

faq.question: How does it handle missing data?
faq.answer: Missing or unverifiable data is explicitly marked as null or 'no_confirmado'. The skill never invents DOIs, h-index values, or publication dates.

faq.question: Can I get output in Spanish?
faq.answer: Yes, the default output language is Spanish. You can request English by setting idioma_salida to 'en'.

faq.question: What is the difference between 'noticia' and 'evidencia_cientifica'?
faq.answer: 'Noticia' refers to the news article (source, date, URL). 'Evidencia_cientifica' is the peer-reviewed or preprint publication linked to that news item, with full academic metadata.

faq.question: How many findings will I get?
faq.answer: Brief depth: 3-5 findings. Medium depth: 5-8 findings. Deep depth: 8-12 findings. Each finding includes 0-2 linked scientific publications.
